study.journal: Barrot L, Asfar P, Mauny F, et al. Liberal or Conservative Oxygen Therapy for Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome. N Engl J Med. 2020;382:999-1008.

population_context.population_description: Patients with ARDS who underwent intubation and received mechanical ventilation for less than 12 hours, with a PaO2:FiO2 ratio of 300 mm Hg or less and bilateral opacities on chest imaging.
population_context.exclusions: Long-term oxygen therapy or noninvasive ventilation at home, cardiac arrest, traumatic brain injury, or cranial hypertension as reason for ICU hospitalization

intervention.name: Conservative oxygen therapy
components: Target PaO2 between 55 and 70 mm Hg
FiO2 adjustment by 0.05 if difference from target less than 5 mm Hg, or by 0.10 if difference greater than 5 mm Hg
SpO2 monitoring every 5 minutes with FiO2 modifications until target reached
intervention.parameter_values: PaO2 55-70 mm Hg; SpO2 88-92%; FiO2 adjustments of 0.05-0.10 based on deviation from target
intervention.timing: First 7 days of invasive mechanical ventilation or until extubation, whichever occurred earlier

comparator.description: Liberal oxygen therapy with target PaO2 between 90 and 105 mm Hg and SpO2 ≥96%

outcome_effect.outcome_name: Death from any cause at 28 days

strengths: Multicenter randomized controlled trial with prospective design
Stratified randomization by center, age, and severity of respiratory failure
Standardized mechanical ventilation protocol applied to both groups
Independent data and safety monitoring board oversight
Assessment of multiple secondary outcomes including organ failure and complications
limitations: Trial was prematurely stopped after enrollment of only 205 patients (vs planned 850), reducing statistical power
Open-label design due to inability to mask oxygen therapy and monitoring parameters
Early termination due to safety concerns limits ability to assess long-term efficacy
Imbalance in mortality at 90 days suggests potential harm from conservative strategy
adverse_events: Five mesenteric ischemic events occurred in the conservative-oxygen group
Higher 90-day mortality in conservative-oxygen group (44.4%) compared to liberal-oxygen group (30.4%)

applicability.generalizability_notes: Findings apply to patients with ARDS requiring mechanical ventilation for less than 12 hours in ICU settings. The safety concerns identified, particularly mesenteric ischemia and increased 90-day mortality, suggest conservative oxygenation may not be appropriate for early ARDS management despite theoretical rationale.

quoted_sentences.design: In this multicenter, randomized trial, we assigned patients with ARDS to receive either conservative oxygen therapy (target Pao2, 55 to 70 mm Hg; oxygen saturation as measured by pulse oximetry [Spo2], 88 to 92%) or liberal oxygen therapy (target Pao2, 90 to 105 mm Hg; Spo2, ≥96%) for 7 days.
quoted_sentences.intervention: Patients were assigned to either the liberal-oxygen group (Pao2 target between 90 and 105 mm Hg) or the conservative-oxygen group (Pao2 target between 55 and 70 mm Hg) over the first 7 days of invasive mechanical ventilation or until extubation, if the latter was performed earlier.
quoted_sentences.results: At day 28, a total of 34 of 99 patients (34.3%) in the conservative-oxygen group and 27 of 102 patients (26.5%) in the liberal-oxygen group had died (difference, 7.8 percentage points; 95% confidence interval [CI], −4.8 to 20.6). At day 90, 44.4% of the patients in the conservative-oxygen group and 30.4% of the patients in the liberal-oxygen group had died (difference, 14.0 percentage points; 95% CI, 0.7 to 27.2). Five mesenteric ischemic events occurred in the conservative-oxygen group.
quoted_sentences.outcome: Among patients with ARDS, early exposure to a conservative-oxygenation strategy with a Pao2 between 55 and 70 mm Hg did not increase survival at 28 days.

METHODS mentary Appendix, available at NEJM.org.
In this multicenter, randomized trial, we assigned patients with ARDS to receive
N Engl J Med 2020;382:999-1008.
either conservative oxygen therapy (target Pao, 55 to 70 mm Hg; oxygen saturation DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa1916431
2

2
Pao, 90 to 105 mm Hg; Spo, ≥96%) for 7 days. The same mechanical-ventilation
2 2
strategies were used in both groups. The primary outcome was death from any
cause at 28 days.
RESULTS
After the enrollment of 205 patients, the trial was prematurely stopped by the data
and safety monitoring board because of safety concerns and a low likelihood of a
significant difference between the two groups in the primary outcome. Four pa-
tients who did not meet the eligibility criteria were excluded. At day 28, a total of
34 of 99 patients (34.3%) in the conservative-oxygen group and 27 of 102 patients
(26.5%) in the liberal-oxygen group had died (difference, 7.8 percentage points;
95% confidence interval [CI], −4.8 to 20.6). At day 90, 44.4% of the patients in the
conservative-oxygen group and 30.4% of the patients in the liberal-oxygen group
had died (difference, 14.0 percentage points; 95% CI, 0.7 to 27.2). Five mesenteric
ischemic events occurred in the conservative-oxygen group.
CONCLUSIONS
Among patients with ARDS, early exposure to a conservative-oxygenation strategy
with a Pao between 55 and 70 mm Hg did not increase survival at 2
_title: Liberal or Conservative Oxygen Therapy for Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome
